volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 2.1.382 - Section 2.2.457c)

sections.label: Section 2.1.382
sections.original: Ecce autem iterum hic deposivit caput et dormit . suscita .
sections.translation: Why look , he has laid down his head and gone to sleep . Do arouse him .

sections.label: Section 2.1.383
sections.original: Etiam vigilas ? pater , inquam , aderit iam hic meus .
sections.translation: Will you awake now ? My father , I tell you , will be here this instant .

sections.label: Section 2.1.383b
sections.translation: How say you ? Your father ? Give me my shoes , that I may take up arms . On my word , I ’ ll kill your father this instant .

sections.label: Section 2.1.385
sections.translation: You ’ re spoiling the whole business ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.385b
sections.translation: do hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 2.1.385c
sections.original: Abripite hunc intro actutum inter manus .
sections.translation: ( To DELPHIUM .) Prithee , do carry him off in your arms into the house .

sections.label: Section 2.1.386
sections.original: Iam hercle ego vos pro matula habebo , nisi mihi matulam datis .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I ’ ll be making an utensil of you just now , if you don ’ t find me one .

sections.label: Section 2.1.387
sections.translation: I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 2.1.387b
sections.original: Habe bonum animum : ego istum lepide medicabo metum .
sections.translation: Be of good courage ; I ’ ll cleverly find a remedy for this alarm .

sections.label: Section 2.1.388
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly ruined !

sections.label: Section 2.1.388b
sections.original: Taceas : ego qui istaec sedem meditabor tibi .
sections.translation: Do hold your tongue ; I ’ ll think of something by means of which to alleviate this for you . Are you satisfied , if on his arrival I shall so manage your father ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.390
sections.original: non modo ne intro eat , verum etiam ut fugiat longe ab aedibus ?
sections.translation: not only that he shall not enter , but even that he shall run away to a distance from the house ? Do you only be off from here in-doors , and remove these things from here with all haste .

sections.label: Section 2.1.392
sections.translation: Where am I to be ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.392b
sections.original: Vbi maxime esse vis : cum hác , cum istac eris .
sections.translation: Where you especially desire : with her ( pointing to PHILEMATIUM ) ; with this girl , too , you ’ ll be . ( Pointing to DELPHEIUM .)

sections.label: Section 2.1.393
sections.original: Quid si igitur abeamus hinc nos ?
sections.translation: How then ? Are we to go away from here ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.393b
sections.translation: Not far from here , Delphium . For carouse away in the house not a bit the less on account of this .

sections.label: Section 2.1.395
sections.original: Ei mihi , quam ístaec blanda dicta quo evenant madeo metu .
sections.translation: Ah me ! I ’ m in a sweat with fear as to how these fine words are to end !

sections.label: Section 2.1.396
sections.original: Potin animó ut sis quieto et facias quod iubeo ?
sections.translation: Can you not be tranquil in your mind , and do as I bid you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.396b
sections.translation: I can be .

sections.label: Section 2.1.397
sections.original: Omnium primum , Philematium , intro abi , et tu , Delphium .
sections.translation: In the first place of all , Philematium , do you go in-doors ; and you , Delphium .

sections.label: Section 2.1.398
sections.translation: We ’ ll both be obedient to you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.398b
sections.translation: May Jupiter grant it so ! Now then , do you give attention as to what I ’ d have attended to .

sections.label: Section 2.1.400
sections.original: omnium primumdum aédes iam face occlusae sient ;
sections.translation: In the first place , then , before anything , cause the house to be shut up at once . Take care and don ’ t let any one whisper a word indoors .

sections.label: Section 2.1.401b
sections.translation: Care shall be taken .

sections.label: Section 2.1.402
sections.original: Tamquam si intus natus nemo in aedibus habitet .
sections.translation: Just as though no living being were dwelling within the house .

sections.label: Section 2.1.402b
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 2.1.403
sections.original: Neu quisquam responset , quando hasce aedis pultabit senex .
sections.translation: And let no one answer , when the old gentleman knocks at the door .

sections.label: Section 2.1.404
sections.translation: Anything else ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.404b
sections.translation: Order the master-key [*] of the house

sections.label: Section 2.1.405
sections.original: iam iube efferri intus : hasce ego aedis occludam hinc foris .
sections.translation: to be brought me at once from within ; this house I ’ ll lock here on the outside .

sections.label: Section 2.1.406
sections.original: In tuam cústodelam meque et meas spes trado , Tranio .—
sections.translation: To your charge I commit myself , Tranio , and my hopes . ( He goes into the house , and the things are removed from the stage .)

sections.label: Section 2.1.407
sections.original: Pluma haud interest , patronus an cliens probior siet .
sections.translation: It matters not a feather whether a patron or a dependant is the nearest at hand for that man who has got no courage in his breast .

sections.label: Section 2.1.410
sections.original: nam cuivis homini , vel optumo vel pessumo
sections.translation: For to every man , whether very good or very bad , even at a moment ’ s notice , it is easy to act with craft ; but this must be looked to , this is the duty of a prudent man , that what has been planned and done in craftiness , may all come about smoothly and without mishap ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.415
sections.original: ne quid potiatur , quam ob rem pigeat vivere .
sections.translation: so that he may not have to put up with anything by reason of which he might be loth to live ; just as I shall manage , that , from the confusion which we shall here create , all shall really go on smoothly and tranquilly , and not produce us any inconvenience in the results . ( Enter a BOY , from the house .) But , why have you come out ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.419b
sections.translation: I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 2.1.419c
sections.translation: ( The BOY shows him the key .) O very well , you ’ ve obeyed my orders most opportunely .

sections.label: Section 2.1.420b
sections.translation: He bade me most earnestly to entreat you some way or other to scare away his father , that he may not enter the house .

sections.label: Section 2.1.422b
sections.translation: Even more , tell him this , that I ’ ll cause that he shan ’ t venture even to look at the house , and to take to flight , covering up his head [*] with the greatest alarm .

sections.label: Section 2.1.425
sections.original: clavem cedo atque abi intro atque occlude ostium ,
sections.translation: Give me the key ( taking it ) , and be off in-doors , and shut to the door , and I ’ ll lock it on this side . ( The BOY goes into the house , and TRANIO locks the door .) Bid him now come forthwith . For the old gentleman here while still alive this day will I institute games [*] in his presence , such as I fancy there will never be for him when he ’ s dead . ( Moving away .) I ’ ll go away from the door to this spot ; hence , I ’ ll look out afar

sections.label: Section 2.1.430
sections.translation: in which direction to lay the burden on the old fellow on his arrival . ( Exit to a little distance .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.431
sections.translation: Neptune , I do return extreme thanks to thee that thou hast just dismissed me from thee , though scarce alive . But if , from this time forward , thou shalt only know that I have stirred a foot upon the main , there is no reason why , that instant ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.435
sections.original: quod nunc voluisti facere quin facias mihi .
sections.translation: thou shouldst not do with me that which thou hast now wished to do . Away with you , away with you from me henceforth for ever after to-day ; what I was to entrust to thee , all of it have I now entrusted . ( overhearing him .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.438
sections.translation: By my troth , Neptune , you ’ ve been much to blame , to have lost this opportunity so fair .

sections.label: Section 2.2.440
sections.translation: After three years , I ’ ve arrived home from Aegypt . I shall come a welcome guest to my household , I suppose .

sections.label: Section 2.2.442
sections.translation: Upon my faith , he might have come a much more welcome one , who had brought the tidings you were dead .

sections.label: Section 2.2.444
sections.original: Sed quid hoc ? occlusa ianua est interdius .
sections.translation: But what means this ? Is the door shut in the daytime ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.445
sections.original: pultabo . heus , ecquis intust ? aperitin fores ?
sections.translation: I ’ ll knock . ( Knocks at the door .) Hallo , there ! is any one going to open this door for me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.446
sections.original: Quis homo est , qui nostras aedes accessit prope ?
sections.translation: What person is it that has come so near to our house ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.447
sections.original: Meus sérvos hic quidem est Tranio .
sections.translation: Surely this is my servant Tranio .

sections.label: Section 2.2.447b
sections.translation: O Theuropides , my master , welcome ; I ’ m glad that you ’ ve arrived in safety . Haveyou been well all along ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.449b
sections.translation: All along , as you see .

sections.label: Section 2.2.449c
sections.translation: That ’ s very good .

sections.label: Section 2.2.450
sections.translation: What about yourselves ? Are you all mad ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.450b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.450c
sections.translation: For this reason ; because you are walking about outside ; not a born person is keeping watch in the house , either to open or to give an answer . With kicking with my feet I ’ ve almost broken in the pannels ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.454_455
sections.original: Eho an tú tetigisti has aedis ?
sections.translation: How now ? Have you been touching this house ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.454_455b
sections.translation: Why shouldn ’ t I touch it ? Why , with kicking it , I tell you , I ’ ve almost broken down the door .

sections.label: Section 2.2.457
sections.translation: What , you touched it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.457b
sections.translation: I touched it , I tell you , and knocked at it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.457c
sections.translation: Out upon you !
